revision.comment: Bot: fix ill template destination links
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園韓神社]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園韓神社]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園韓神社]]
[[File:Imperial palace.svg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Location of {{ill|Sonokarakami Shrine|ja|園韓神社|1=Sono & Kara Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422896}} in the {{ill|Daidairi|ja|大内裏|1=Heian Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304501}} of {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|1=Heian-kyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q751907}}}}]]
'''Sonokarakami Shrine''' (そのからかみのやしろ / Sonokarakami no yashiro, also Sonokarakami-sha) is a collective name for '''Sono Shrine''' (そのじんじゃ / Sono jinja, also 薗神社 ''Sono Jinja'') and '''Kara Shrine''' (からじんじゃ / Kara jinja, ''Karakami no yashiro''). It was a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} ({{ill|Myōjin taisha|ja|名神大社|1=Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9610964}}) that is now defunct. It may have been succeeded by enshrinement in the ''Shinden'' of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿}}.

== Overview ==
Both Sono Shrine and Kara Shrine were {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrines|1=Shinto shrine}} that stood within the {{ill|宮内省|ja|宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}} of the imperial court ({{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|1=Heian-kyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q751907}}’s {{ill|Daidairi|ja|大内裏|1=Heian Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304501}}) until around the time of the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|1=Ōnin War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q385676}}.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} In the ''{{ill|Shūgaishō|ja|拾芥抄|1=Shūgaishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11497878}}'' palace plan they appear west of the Ministry of the Imperial Household, while in the ''{{ill|Nenjū Gyōji Emaki|ja|年中行事絵巻|1=Nenjū Gyōji Emaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17635666}}'' they appear at the ministry’s north-west corner; this spot corresponds to the north-east of Chikara-chō (主税町) in today’s {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|1=Kyoto Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q120730}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|1=Kyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q34600}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|上京区|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1321824}}.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} The shrine buildings were two halls aligned north–south (Sono Shrine to the south, Kara Shrine to the north), both facing east, each in a {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}-like style with one bay across the front and two bays on the sides.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

They were the only {{ill|Myōjin taisha|ja|名神大社|1=Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9610964}} within the palace precincts, and in the Heian period their {{ill|annual festival|ja|例祭|1=Reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}}—the “Sonokarakami no Matsuri”—was prescribed to be held twice a year, showing that the court regarded them as important. However, after the Ōnin War they were abolished, and to this day there are various theories regarding their divine identities and origins.

== Enshrined deities ==
As for the deity Sono-no-kami, there is no mention in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', etc., and the identity is unknown.{{Sfn|宮内省坐神三座（式内社）|1979年}} One theory says it was the deity guarding the garden pond (''sonochi'') of the Ministry of the Imperial Household.{{Sfn|宮内省坐神三座（式内社）|1979年}} Regarding Kara-no-kami, the ''Kojiki'' lists five divine offspring ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}}) of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3028663}} and Inohime (daughter of {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神活須毘神}}): {{ill|Ōkuni Mitama no Kami|ja|大国御魂神}}, {{ill|Kara no Kami|ja|韓神}}, Sofuri no Kami ({{ill|曾富理神|ja|曾富理神}}), Shirahi no Kami ({{ill|白日神|ja|白日神}}), and {{ill|Hijiri no Kami|ja|聖神（日本神話）}}.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} (There is also a theory that this Kara no Kami is a different deity.{{Sfn|宮内省坐神三座（式内社）|1979年}}) {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|1=Motoori Norinaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q384647}}, in his ''{{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝|1=Kojiki-den|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410835}}'', proposed that Sofuri no Kami is identical with one of the two deities Sono-no-kami or Kara-no-kami.{{Sfn|園韓神祭（国史）}}

According to the ''Daiwa Jinja Chūshinjō'' (大倭神社註進状), Sono Shrine enshrined {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, while Kara Shrine enshrined {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}}; it further states that these deities were descendants of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q272993}} and protected against epidemics.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

== History ==
=== Foundation ===
The ''Daiwa Jinja Chūshinjō'', quoting the ''Ōgami Ujikechō'' (大神氏家牒), states that the shrines were founded by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} in the {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老|1=Yōrō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373061}} ({{ill|717|ja|717年|1=717|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23532}}–{{ill|724|ja|724年|1=724|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23549}}).{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

On the other hand, according to the ''{{ill|Goke Shidai|ja|江家次第|1=Q11550820|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550820}}'' and ''{{ill|Kojidan|ja|古事談|1=Kojidan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3013995}}'', when it was planned to relocate the Sonokarakami Shrine at the time of the move to {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|1=Heian-kyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q751907}}, a divine oracle declared: “We will remain here to protect the emperor,” and so the relocation was cancelled.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} From this account it is thought that from its foundation the shrine stood near the Ministry of the Imperial Household, and it is said to be the oldest among the 36 deities of the palace.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} Because the area before the relocation to Heian-kyō had been governed by the immigrant {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|1=Hata clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q987290}}, there is also a theory that Sono-no-kami and Kara-no-kami were originally deities venerated by the Hata. <ref name="秦氏">{{ill|Chiaki Mizutani|ja|水谷千秋|1=Chiaki Mizutani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549157}}『謎の渡来人秦氏（文春新書）』{{ill|Bungeishunjū|ja|文藝春秋|1=Bungeishunjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q906927}}, 2009, pp. 206–212.</ref>

[[File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kankoku Shrine|ja|漢國神社|1=Kangō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11565493}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|1=Nara Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131287}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}})}}{{Smaller|According to its shrine legend, it is the source of the Sonokarakami Shrine’s enshrinement.}}]]
In another account, the shrine legend of {{ill|Kankoku Shrine|ja|漢國神社|1=Kangō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11565493}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|1=Nara Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131287}} says that during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko|2=ja|3=推古天皇|ja|推古天皇|1=Suiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q298057}} Ōgami no Kimi Shirotsutsumi (大神君白堤) enshrined Ōmononushi as Sono-no-kami, and later, in the Yōrō era, {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|ja|藤原不比等|1=Fujiwara no Fuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177517}} enshrined Ōkuninushi and Sukunabikona as Kara-no-kami.{{Sfn|漢国神社（国史）}} It further states that the imperial court’s enshrinement of these deities became the foundation of the Sonokarakami Shrine.{{Sfn|漢国神社（国史）}}

=== Outline history ===
In documents, the earliest mention is in the ''{{ill|Shinsō kyakuchiku shō|ja|新抄格勅符抄|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502312}}''<ref group="原">''Shinsō kyakuchiku shō'' 10, “Shinji Shoke Fuko,” Daidō 1 (806) document.</ref>, which records that in {{ill|Tenpyō-jingo|ja|天平神護|1=Tenpyō-jingo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373181}} 1 ({{ill|765|ja|765年|1=765|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31216}}) 20 households of {{ill|shinpu|ja|神封|1=Shinpo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1689534}} (fief households) were allotted from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|1=Sanuki Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q929682}} to Sono-no-kami and 10 to Kara-no-kami. The ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}''<ref group="原" name="延暦20年"/> notes the Sonokarakami festival as an {{ill|annual festival|ja|例祭|1=Reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|1=Enryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373176}} 20 ({{ill|801|ja|801年|1=801|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30008}}).

In the official histories, in {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥|1=Kashō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373157}} 3 ({{ill|850|ja|850年|1=850|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30364}}){{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}<ref group="原" name="嘉祥3年"/> the shrines were granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower; in {{ill|Saikō|ja|斉衡|1=Saikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1324664}} 1 ({{ill|854|ja|854年|1=854|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24981}}){{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}<ref group="原" name="斉衡元年"/> they were raised to Junior Third Rank; in Saikō 2 ({{ill|855|ja|855年|1=855|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25073}}){{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}<ref group="原" name="斉衡2年"/> they were listed as {{ill|myōjin|ja|名神|1=myoujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415523}}; and in {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|1=Jōgan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1075635}} 1 ({{ill|859|ja|859年|1=859|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23897}}){{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}<ref group="原" name="貞観元年"/> they were promoted from Junior Third Rank to Senior Third Rank.

In the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'' ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}}'' compiled in {{ill|927|ja|927年|1=927|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30470}}, under “Three Deities Enshrined in the Ministry of the Imperial Household; all myōjin; for the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516161}} and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501518}},” they are listed as “Sono Shrine” and “Kara Shrine (two seats),” thus placed among the {{ill|Myōjin taisha|ja|名神大社|1=Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9610964}} and entitled to offerings at the Tsukinami and Niiname rites.{{Sfn|宮内省坐神三座（式内社）|1979年}} They are the only two shrines within the palace to be ranked as myōjin taisha. The ''Engishiki'' also stipulates that the shrines’ maintenance expenses come from the sealed households in Sanuki Province, and that one sealed servant (''fuchō'') be assigned to guard the shinden.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

The ''{{ill|Chōya Gunsai|ja|朝野群載|1=Chōya Gunsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517735}}'' records a petition for repairs in {{ill|Eihō|ja|永保|1=Eihō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193881}} 2 ({{ill|1082|ja|1082年|1=1082|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19452}}), from which the dilapidated state of the buildings can be inferred.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} According to the ''{{ill|Chōshūki|ja|長秋記|1=Minamoto no Morotoki's Diary|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653546}}''<ref group="原">''Chōshūki'', Daiji 5 (1130) 11/14 entry.</ref>, by around {{ill|Daiji (Japan)|ja|大治 (日本)|1=Daiji (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1037046}} 5 ({{ill|1130|ja|1130年|1=1130|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19562}}) the ancient form of the rites had been lost.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} The ''{{ill|Yasutomi-ki|ja|康富記|1=Yasutomi-ki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486253}}''<ref group="原">''Yasutomi-ki'', Ōei 26 (1419) 2/14 entry.</ref> notes that on the 5th day of the 2nd month of {{ill|Ōei|ja|応永|1=Ōei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193078}} 26 ({{ill|1419|ja|1419年|1=1419|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6205}}) a great wind toppled the shrine buildings.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} Thereafter, since the inner palace was destroyed in the Ōnin War, the Sonokarakami Shrine is likewise thought to have been abolished.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

It is unclear how the rites were continued after abolition, but it is thought that, together with the other palace deities, Sonokarakami is now venerated among the “Tenjin Chigi” in the ''Shinden'' of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿}} at the Imperial Palace. <ref>''Shikinai-sha Chōsa Hōkoku'' vol. 1, Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai ed., Kogakkan University Press, 1979, pp. 28–29.</ref>

=== Ranks ===
* {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥|1=Kashō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373157}} 3 ({{ill|850|ja|850年|1=850|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30364}}) 10/20: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower (''{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}'').<ref group="原" name="嘉祥3年">''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', Kashō 3 (850) 10th month, ''kinoe-ne'' (20th) entry (see {{Harvnb|薗神社（集成）}}; {{Harvnb|韓神社二座（集成）}}).</ref> — written as “Shrines of Sono-kami, Kara-kami, etc.”
* {{ill|Saikō|ja|斉衡|1=Saikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1324664}} 1 ({{ill|854|ja|854年|1=854|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24981}}) 4/9: Junior Third Rank (''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'').<ref group="原" name="斉衡元年">''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', Saikō 1 (854) 4th month, ''mizunoto-i'' (9th) entry (see {{Harvnb|薗神社（集成）}}; {{Harvnb|韓神社二座（集成）}}).</ref> — written as “Sono-kami” and “Kara-kami.”
* Saikō 2 ({{ill|855|ja|855年|1=855|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25073}}) 9/9: Listed as myōjin (''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'').<ref group="原" name="斉衡2年">''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', Saikō 2 (855) 9th month, ''mizunoto-chū'' (9th) entry (see {{Harvnb|薗神社（集成）}}; {{Harvnb|韓神社二座（集成）}}).</ref> — written as “Sono-kami” and “Kara-kami.”
* {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|1=Jōgan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1075635}} 1 ({{ill|859|ja|859年|1=859|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23897}}) 1/27: From Junior Third Rank to Senior Third Rank (''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'').<ref group="原" name="貞観元年">''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', Jōgan 1 (859) 1/27 entry (see {{Harvnb|薗神社（集成）}}; {{Harvnb|韓神社二座（集成）}}).</ref> — written as “Sono-kami” and “Kara-kami.”

== Rites ==
=== Sonokarakami Festival ===
The {{ill|annual festival|ja|例祭|1=Reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} of the Sonokarakami Shrine was called the “'''Sonokarakami no Matsuri'''.”{{Sfn|園韓神祭（国史）}} It was prescribed to be held twice a year: on the {{ill|Ox (zodiac)|ja|丑|1=chǒu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16539206}} day after the {{ill|Kasuga Festival|ja|春日祭|1=Kasuga-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513681}} in the 2nd month, and on the Ox day before the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501518}} in the 11th month.{{Sfn|園韓神祭（国史）}} The first record is from {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|1=Enryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373176}} 20 ({{ill|801|ja|801年|1=801|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30008}}),<ref group="原" name="延暦20年">''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku'' 1 “Keharaigoto,” Enryaku 20 (801) 5/14 order (see {{Harvnb|薗神社（集成）}}; {{Harvnb|韓神社二座（集成）}}).</ref><ref name="秦氏"/> and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that it was performed regularly.

The content of the rite is described in detail in the ''{{ill|Jōgan Gishiki|ja|貞観儀式|1=Jōgan Gishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634544}}'', etc.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} On the day, two ''kambe'' (神部, shrine officials) erected a sacred tree (''sakaki'') in the courtyard, lit a ritual fire, and the ''miko'' (御巫) recited norito. After the recitation, flute and ''koto'' were played, the ''mikoko'' (御神子) danced a ''yudate'' dance circling the fire, and then eight ''kambe'' danced together.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} The same sequence was then performed first at Sono Shrine, then at Kara Shrine, and afterwards a ''wa-mai'' dance was performed again at Sono Shrine.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} After the ministers and above had withdrawn, the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2264720}} performed singing and dancing (''kagura'') together with the ''miko'', ''monoimi'', and ''kambe'' in front of both sanctuaries.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} This ''kagura'' by the Jingikan, predating the ''Naishidokoro'' Kagura established in the time of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇|1=Emperor Ichijō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q449540}}, is considered a type of court ''kagura''.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

The ''Goke Shidai'' says four ''kambe'' danced holding ''sakaki'', halberds, bows, and swords, while the ''{{ill|Hyakurenshō|ja|百錬抄|1=Hyakurenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580870}}'' records that when they tried to retrieve the true images (''go-shōtai'') of the Sonokarakami during the Daidairi fire of {{ill|Daiji (Japan)|ja|大治 (日本)|1=Daiji (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1037046}} 2 ({{ill|1127|ja|1127年|1=1127|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23827}}), there were sacred treasures consisting of swords and halberds.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} Thereafter, the festival is said to have gradually declined after the late Heian period.{{Sfn|園韓神祭（国史）}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園韓神社]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園韓神社]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園韓神社]]
[[File:Imperial palace.svg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Location of {{ill|Sonokarakami Shrine|ja|園韓神社|1=Sono & Kara Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422896}} in the {{ill|Daidairi|ja|大内裏|1=Heian Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304501}} of {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|1=Heian-kyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q751907}}}}]]
'''Sonokarakami Shrine''' (そのからかみのやしろ / Sonokarakami no yashiro, also Sonokarakami-sha) is a collective name for '''Sono Shrine''' (そのじんじゃ / Sono jinja, also 薗神社 ''Sono Jinja'') and '''Kara Shrine''' (からじんじゃ / Kara jinja, ''Karakami no yashiro''). It was a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} ({{ill|Myōjin taisha|ja|名神大社|1=Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9610964}}) that is now defunct. It may have been succeeded by enshrinement in the ''Shinden'' of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿}}.

== Overview ==
Both Sono Shrine and Kara Shrine were {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrines|1=Shinto shrine}} that stood within the {{ill|宮内省|ja|宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}} of the imperial court ({{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|1=Heian-kyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q751907}}’s {{ill|Daidairi|ja|大内裏|1=Heian Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304501}}) until around the time of the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|1=Ōnin War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q385676}}.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} In the ''{{ill|Shūgaishō|ja|拾芥抄|1=Shūgaishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11497878}}'' palace plan they appear west of the Ministry of the Imperial Household, while in the ''{{ill|Nenjū Gyōji Emaki|ja|年中行事絵巻|1=Nenjū Gyōji Emaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17635666}}'' they appear at the ministry’s north-west corner; this spot corresponds to the north-east of Chikara-chō (主税町) in today’s {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|1=Kyoto Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q120730}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|1=Kyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q34600}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|上京区|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1321824}}.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} The shrine buildings were two halls aligned north–south (Sono Shrine to the south, Kara Shrine to the north), both facing east, each in a {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}-like style with one bay across the front and two bays on the sides.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

They were the only {{ill|Myōjin taisha|ja|名神大社|1=Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9610964}} within the palace precincts, and in the Heian period their {{ill|annual festival|ja|例祭|1=Reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}}—the “Sonokarakami no Matsuri”—was prescribed to be held twice a year, showing that the court regarded them as important. However, after the Ōnin War they were abolished, and to this day there are various theories regarding their divine identities and origins.

== Enshrined deities ==
As for the deity Sono-no-kami, there is no mention in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', etc., and the identity is unknown.{{Sfn|宮内省坐神三座（式内社）|1979年}} One theory says it was the deity guarding the garden pond (''sonochi'') of the Ministry of the Imperial Household.{{Sfn|宮内省坐神三座（式内社）|1979年}} Regarding Kara-no-kami, the ''Kojiki'' lists five divine offspring ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}}) of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3028663}} and Inohime (daughter of {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神活須毘神}}): {{ill|Ōkuni Mitama no Kami|ja|大国御魂神}}, {{ill|Kara no Kami|ja|韓神}}, Sofuri no Kami ({{ill|曾富理神|ja|曾富理神}}), Shirahi no Kami ({{ill|白日神|ja|白日神}}), and {{ill|Hijiri no Kami|ja|聖神（日本神話）}}.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} (There is also a theory that this Kara no Kami is a different deity.{{Sfn|宮内省坐神三座（式内社）|1979年}}) {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|1=Motoori Norinaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q384647}}, in his ''{{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝|1=Kojiki-den|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410835}}'', proposed that Sofuri no Kami is identical with one of the two deities Sono-no-kami or Kara-no-kami.{{Sfn|園韓神祭（国史）}}

According to the ''Daiwa Jinja Chūshinjō'' (大倭神社註進状), Sono Shrine enshrined {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, while Kara Shrine enshrined {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}}; it further states that these deities were descendants of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q272993}} and protected against epidemics.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

== History ==
=== Foundation ===
The ''Daiwa Jinja Chūshinjō'', quoting the ''Ōgami Ujikechō'' (大神氏家牒), states that the shrines were founded by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} in the {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老|1=Yōrō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373061}} ({{ill|717|ja|717年|1=717|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23532}}–{{ill|724|ja|724年|1=724|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23549}}).{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

On the other hand, according to the ''{{ill|Goke Shidai|ja|江家次第|1=Q11550820|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550820}}'' and ''{{ill|Kojidan|ja|古事談|1=Kojidan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3013995}}'', when it was planned to relocate the Sonokarakami Shrine at the time of the move to {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|1=Heian-kyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q751907}}, a divine oracle declared: “We will remain here to protect the emperor,” and so the relocation was cancelled.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} From this account it is thought that from its foundation the shrine stood near the Ministry of the Imperial Household, and it is said to be the oldest among the 36 deities of the palace.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} Because the area before the relocation to Heian-kyō had been governed by the immigrant {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|1=Hata clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q987290}}, there is also a theory that Sono-no-kami and Kara-no-kami were originally deities venerated by the Hata. <ref name="秦氏">{{ill|Chiaki Mizutani|ja|水谷千秋|1=Chiaki Mizutani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549157}}『謎の渡来人秦氏（文春新書）』{{ill|Bungeishunjū|ja|文藝春秋|1=Bungeishunjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q906927}}, 2009, pp. 206–212.</ref>

[[File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kankoku Shrine|ja|漢國神社|1=Kangō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11565493}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|1=Nara Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131287}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}})}}{{Smaller|According to its shrine legend, it is the source of the Sonokarakami Shrine’s enshrinement.}}]]
In another account, the shrine legend of {{ill|Kankoku Shrine|ja|漢國神社|1=Kangō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11565493}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|1=Nara Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131287}} says that during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko|2=ja|3=推古天皇|ja|推古天皇|1=Suiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q298057}} Ōgami no Kimi Shirotsutsumi (大神君白堤) enshrined Ōmononushi as Sono-no-kami, and later, in the Yōrō era, {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|ja|藤原不比等|1=Fujiwara no Fuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177517}} enshrined Ōkuninushi and Sukunabikona as Kara-no-kami.{{Sfn|漢国神社（国史）}} It further states that the imperial court’s enshrinement of these deities became the foundation of the Sonokarakami Shrine.{{Sfn|漢国神社（国史）}}

=== Outline history ===
In documents, the earliest mention is in the ''{{ill|Shinsō kyakuchiku shō|ja|新抄格勅符抄|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502312}}''<ref group="原">''Shinsō kyakuchiku shō'' 10, “Shinji Shoke Fuko,” Daidō 1 (806) document.</ref>, which records that in {{ill|Tenpyō-jingo|ja|天平神護|1=Tenpyō-jingo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373181}} 1 ({{ill|765|ja|765年|1=765|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31216}}) 20 households of {{ill|shinpu|ja|神封|1=Shinpo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1689534}} (fief households) were allotted from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|1=Sanuki Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q929682}} to Sono-no-kami and 10 to Kara-no-kami. The ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}''<ref group="原" name="延暦20年"/> notes the Sonokarakami festival as an {{ill|annual festival|ja|例祭|1=Reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|1=Enryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373176}} 20 ({{ill|801|ja|801年|1=801|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30008}}).

In the official histories, in {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥|1=Kashō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373157}} 3 ({{ill|850|ja|850年|1=850|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30364}}){{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}<ref group="原" name="嘉祥3年"/> the shrines were granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower; in {{ill|Saikō|ja|斉衡|1=Saikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1324664}} 1 ({{ill|854|ja|854年|1=854|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24981}}){{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}<ref group="原" name="斉衡元年"/> they were raised to Junior Third Rank; in Saikō 2 ({{ill|855|ja|855年|1=855|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25073}}){{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}<ref group="原" name="斉衡2年"/> they were listed as {{ill|myōjin|ja|名神|1=myoujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415523}}; and in {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|1=Jōgan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1075635}} 1 ({{ill|859|ja|859年|1=859|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23897}}){{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}<ref group="原" name="貞観元年"/> they were promoted from Junior Third Rank to Senior Third Rank.

In the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'' ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}}'' compiled in {{ill|927|ja|927年|1=927|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30470}}, under “Three Deities Enshrined in the Ministry of the Imperial Household; all myōjin; for the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516161}} and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501518}},” they are listed as “Sono Shrine” and “Kara Shrine (two seats),” thus placed among the {{ill|Myōjin taisha|ja|名神大社|1=Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9610964}} and entitled to offerings at the Tsukinami and Niiname rites.{{Sfn|宮内省坐神三座（式内社）|1979年}} They are the only two shrines within the palace to be ranked as myōjin taisha. The ''Engishiki'' also stipulates that the shrines’ maintenance expenses come from the sealed households in Sanuki Province, and that one sealed servant (''fuchō'') be assigned to guard the shinden.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

The ''{{ill|Chōya Gunsai|ja|朝野群載|1=Chōya Gunsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517735}}'' records a petition for repairs in {{ill|Eihō|ja|永保|1=Eihō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193881}} 2 ({{ill|1082|ja|1082年|1=1082|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19452}}), from which the dilapidated state of the buildings can be inferred.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} According to the ''{{ill|Chōshūki|ja|長秋記|1=Minamoto no Morotoki's Diary|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653546}}''<ref group="原">''Chōshūki'', Daiji 5 (1130) 11/14 entry.</ref>, by around {{ill|Daiji (Japan)|ja|大治 (日本)|1=Daiji (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1037046}} 5 ({{ill|1130|ja|1130年|1=1130|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19562}}) the ancient form of the rites had been lost.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} The ''{{ill|Yasutomi-ki|ja|康富記|1=Yasutomi-ki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486253}}''<ref group="原">''Yasutomi-ki'', Ōei 26 (1419) 2/14 entry.</ref> notes that on the 5th day of the 2nd month of {{ill|Ōei|ja|応永|1=Ōei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193078}} 26 ({{ill|1419|ja|1419年|1=1419|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6205}}) a great wind toppled the shrine buildings.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} Thereafter, since the inner palace was destroyed in the Ōnin War, the Sonokarakami Shrine is likewise thought to have been abolished.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

It is unclear how the rites were continued after abolition, but it is thought that, together with the other palace deities, Sonokarakami is now venerated among the “Tenjin Chigi” in the ''Shinden'' of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿}} at the Imperial Palace. <ref>''Shikinai-sha Chōsa Hōkoku'' vol. 1, Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai ed., Kogakkan University Press, 1979, pp. 28–29.</ref>

=== Ranks ===
* {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥|1=Kashō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373157}} 3 ({{ill|850|ja|850年|1=850|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30364}}) 10/20: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower (''{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}'').<ref group="原" name="嘉祥3年">''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', Kashō 3 (850) 10th month, ''kinoe-ne'' (20th) entry (see {{Harvnb|薗神社（集成）}}; {{Harvnb|韓神社二座（集成）}}).</ref> — written as “Shrines of Sono-kami, Kara-kami, etc.”
* {{ill|Saikō|ja|斉衡|1=Saikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1324664}} 1 ({{ill|854|ja|854年|1=854|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24981}}) 4/9: Junior Third Rank (''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'').<ref group="原" name="斉衡元年">''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', Saikō 1 (854) 4th month, ''mizunoto-i'' (9th) entry (see {{Harvnb|薗神社（集成）}}; {{Harvnb|韓神社二座（集成）}}).</ref> — written as “Sono-kami” and “Kara-kami.”
* Saikō 2 ({{ill|855|ja|855年|1=855|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25073}}) 9/9: Listed as myōjin (''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'').<ref group="原" name="斉衡2年">''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', Saikō 2 (855) 9th month, ''mizunoto-chū'' (9th) entry (see {{Harvnb|薗神社（集成）}}; {{Harvnb|韓神社二座（集成）}}).</ref> — written as “Sono-kami” and “Kara-kami.”
* {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|1=Jōgan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1075635}} 1 ({{ill|859|ja|859年|1=859|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23897}}) 1/27: From Junior Third Rank to Senior Third Rank (''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'').<ref group="原" name="貞観元年">''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', Jōgan 1 (859) 1/27 entry (see {{Harvnb|薗神社（集成）}}; {{Harvnb|韓神社二座（集成）}}).</ref> — written as “Sono-kami” and “Kara-kami.”

== Rites ==
=== Sonokarakami Festival ===
The {{ill|annual festival|ja|例祭|1=Reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} of the Sonokarakami Shrine was called the “'''Sonokarakami no Matsuri'''.”{{Sfn|園韓神祭（国史）}} It was prescribed to be held twice a year: on the {{ill|Ox (zodiac)|ja|丑|1=chǒu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16539206}} day after the {{ill|Kasuga Festival|ja|春日祭|1=Kasuga-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513681}} in the 2nd month, and on the Ox day before the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501518}} in the 11th month.{{Sfn|園韓神祭（国史）}} The first record is from {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|1=Enryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373176}} 20 ({{ill|801|ja|801年|1=801|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30008}}),<ref group="原" name="延暦20年">''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku'' 1 “Keharaigoto,” Enryaku 20 (801) 5/14 order (see {{Harvnb|薗神社（集成）}}; {{Harvnb|韓神社二座（集成）}}).</ref><ref name="秦氏"/> and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that it was performed regularly.

The content of the rite is described in detail in the ''{{ill|Jōgan Gishiki|ja|貞観儀式|1=Jōgan Gishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634544}}'', etc.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} On the day, two ''kambe'' (神部, shrine officials) erected a sacred tree (''sakaki'') in the courtyard, lit a ritual fire, and the ''miko'' (御巫) recited norito. After the recitation, flute and ''koto'' were played, the ''mikoko'' (御神子) danced a ''yudate'' dance circling the fire, and then eight ''kambe'' danced together.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} The same sequence was then performed first at Sono Shrine, then at Kara Shrine, and afterwards a ''wa-mai'' dance was performed again at Sono Shrine.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} After the ministers and above had withdrawn, the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2264720}} performed singing and dancing (''kagura'') together with the ''miko'', ''monoimi'', and ''kambe'' in front of both sanctuaries.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} This ''kagura'' by the Jingikan, predating the ''Naishidokoro'' Kagura established in the time of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇|1=Emperor Ichijō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q449540}}, is considered a type of court ''kagura''.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

The ''Goke Shidai'' says four ''kambe'' danced holding ''sakaki'', halberds, bows, and swords, while the ''{{ill|Hyakurenshō|ja|百錬抄|1=Hyakurenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580870}}'' records that when they tried to retrieve the true images (''go-shōtai'') of the Sonokarakami during the Daidairi fire of {{ill|Daiji (Japan)|ja|大治 (日本)|1=Daiji (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1037046}} 2 ({{ill|1127|ja|1127年|1=1127|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23827}}), there were sacred treasures consisting of swords and halberds.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} Thereafter, the festival is said to have gradually declined after the late Heian period.{{Sfn|園韓神祭（国史）}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園韓神社]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園韓神社]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園韓神社]]
[[File:Imperial palace.svg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Location of {{ill|Sonokarakami Shrine|ja|園韓神社|1=Sono & Kara Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422896}} in the {{ill|Daidairi|ja|大内裏|1=Heian Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304501}} of {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|1=Heian-kyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q751907}}}}]]
'''Sonokarakami Shrine''' (そのからかみのやしろ / Sonokarakami no yashiro, also Sonokarakami-sha) is a collective name for '''Sono Shrine''' (そのじんじゃ / Sono jinja, also 薗神社 ''Sono Jinja'') and '''Kara Shrine''' (からじんじゃ / Kara jinja, ''Karakami no yashiro''). It was a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} ({{ill|Myōjin taisha|ja|名神大社|1=Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9610964}}) that is now defunct. It may have been succeeded by enshrinement in the ''Shinden'' of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿}}.

== Overview ==
Both Sono Shrine and Kara Shrine were {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrines|1=Shinto shrine}} that stood within the {{ill|宮内省|ja|宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}} of the imperial court ({{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|1=Heian-kyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q751907}}’s {{ill|Daidairi|ja|大内裏|1=Heian Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304501}}) until around the time of the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|1=Ōnin War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q385676}}.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} In the ''{{ill|Shūgaishō|ja|拾芥抄|1=Shūgaishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11497878}}'' palace plan they appear west of the Ministry of the Imperial Household, while in the ''{{ill|Nenjū Gyōji Emaki|ja|年中行事絵巻|1=Nenjū Gyōji Emaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17635666}}'' they appear at the ministry’s north-west corner; this spot corresponds to the north-east of Chikara-chō (主税町) in today’s {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|1=Kyoto Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q120730}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|1=Kyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q34600}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|上京区|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1321824}}.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} The shrine buildings were two halls aligned north–south (Sono Shrine to the south, Kara Shrine to the north), both facing east, each in a {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}-like style with one bay across the front and two bays on the sides.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

They were the only {{ill|Myōjin taisha|ja|名神大社|1=Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9610964}} within the palace precincts, and in the Heian period their {{ill|annual festival|ja|例祭|1=Reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}}—the “Sonokarakami no Matsuri”—was prescribed to be held twice a year, showing that the court regarded them as important. However, after the Ōnin War they were abolished, and to this day there are various theories regarding their divine identities and origins.

== Enshrined deities ==
As for the deity Sono-no-kami, there is no mention in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', etc., and the identity is unknown.{{Sfn|宮内省坐神三座（式内社）|1979年}} One theory says it was the deity guarding the garden pond (''sonochi'') of the Ministry of the Imperial Household.{{Sfn|宮内省坐神三座（式内社）|1979年}} Regarding Kara-no-kami, the ''Kojiki'' lists five divine offspring ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}}) of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3028663}} and Inohime (daughter of {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神活須毘神}}): {{ill|Ōkuni Mitama no Kami|ja|大国御魂神}}, {{ill|Kara no Kami|ja|韓神}}, Sofuri no Kami ({{ill|曾富理神|ja|曾富理神}}), Shirahi no Kami ({{ill|白日神|ja|白日神}}), and {{ill|Hijiri no Kami|ja|聖神（日本神話）}}.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} (There is also a theory that this Kara no Kami is a different deity.{{Sfn|宮内省坐神三座（式内社）|1979年}}) {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|1=Motoori Norinaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q384647}}, in his ''{{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝|1=Kojiki-den|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410835}}'', proposed that Sofuri no Kami is identical with one of the two deities Sono-no-kami or Kara-no-kami.{{Sfn|園韓神祭（国史）}}

According to the ''Daiwa Jinja Chūshinjō'' (大倭神社註進状), Sono Shrine enshrined {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, while Kara Shrine enshrined {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}}; it further states that these deities were descendants of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q272993}} and protected against epidemics.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

== History ==
=== Foundation ===
The ''Daiwa Jinja Chūshinjō'', quoting the ''Ōgami Ujikechō'' (大神氏家牒), states that the shrines were founded by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} in the {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老|1=Yōrō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373061}} ({{ill|717|ja|717年|1=717|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23532}}–{{ill|724|ja|724年|1=724|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23549}}).{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

On the other hand, according to the ''{{ill|Goke Shidai|ja|江家次第|1=Q11550820|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550820}}'' and ''{{ill|Kojidan|ja|古事談|1=Kojidan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3013995}}'', when it was planned to relocate the Sonokarakami Shrine at the time of the move to {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|1=Heian-kyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q751907}}, a divine oracle declared: “We will remain here to protect the emperor,” and so the relocation was cancelled.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} From this account it is thought that from its foundation the shrine stood near the Ministry of the Imperial Household, and it is said to be the oldest among the 36 deities of the palace.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} Because the area before the relocation to Heian-kyō had been governed by the immigrant {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|1=Hata clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q987290}}, there is also a theory that Sono-no-kami and Kara-no-kami were originally deities venerated by the Hata. <ref name="秦氏">{{ill|Chiaki Mizutani|ja|水谷千秋|1=Chiaki Mizutani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549157}}『謎の渡来人秦氏（文春新書）』{{ill|Bungeishunjū|ja|文藝春秋|1=Bungeishunjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q906927}}, 2009, pp. 206–212.</ref>

[[File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kankoku Shrine|ja|漢國神社|1=Kangō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11565493}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|1=Nara Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131287}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}})}}{{Smaller|According to its shrine legend, it is the source of the Sonokarakami Shrine’s enshrinement.}}]]
In another account, the shrine legend of {{ill|Kankoku Shrine|ja|漢國神社|1=Kangō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11565493}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|1=Nara Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131287}} says that during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko|2=ja|3=推古天皇|ja|推古天皇|1=Suiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q298057}} Ōgami no Kimi Shirotsutsumi (大神君白堤) enshrined Ōmononushi as Sono-no-kami, and later, in the Yōrō era, {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|ja|藤原不比等|1=Fujiwara no Fuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177517}} enshrined Ōkuninushi and Sukunabikona as Kara-no-kami.{{Sfn|漢国神社（国史）}} It further states that the imperial court’s enshrinement of these deities became the foundation of the Sonokarakami Shrine.{{Sfn|漢国神社（国史）}}

=== Outline history ===
In documents, the earliest mention is in the ''{{ill|Shinsō kyakuchiku shō|ja|新抄格勅符抄|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502312}}''<ref group="原">''Shinsō kyakuchiku shō'' 10, “Shinji Shoke Fuko,” Daidō 1 (806) document.</ref>, which records that in {{ill|Tenpyō-jingo|ja|天平神護|1=Tenpyō-jingo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373181}} 1 ({{ill|765|ja|765年|1=765|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31216}}) 20 households of {{ill|shinpu|ja|神封|1=Shinpo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1689534}} (fief households) were allotted from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|1=Sanuki Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q929682}} to Sono-no-kami and 10 to Kara-no-kami. The ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}''<ref group="原" name="延暦20年"/> notes the Sonokarakami festival as an {{ill|annual festival|ja|例祭|1=Reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|1=Enryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373176}} 20 ({{ill|801|ja|801年|1=801|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30008}}).

In the official histories, in {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥|1=Kashō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373157}} 3 ({{ill|850|ja|850年|1=850|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30364}}){{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}<ref group="原" name="嘉祥3年"/> the shrines were granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower; in {{ill|Saikō|ja|斉衡|1=Saikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1324664}} 1 ({{ill|854|ja|854年|1=854|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24981}}){{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}<ref group="原" name="斉衡元年"/> they were raised to Junior Third Rank; in Saikō 2 ({{ill|855|ja|855年|1=855|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25073}}){{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}<ref group="原" name="斉衡2年"/> they were listed as {{ill|myōjin|ja|名神|1=myoujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415523}}; and in {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|1=Jōgan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1075635}} 1 ({{ill|859|ja|859年|1=859|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23897}}){{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}<ref group="原" name="貞観元年"/> they were promoted from Junior Third Rank to Senior Third Rank.

In the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'' ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}}'' compiled in {{ill|927|ja|927年|1=927|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30470}}, under “Three Deities Enshrined in the Ministry of the Imperial Household; all myōjin; for the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516161}} and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501518}},” they are listed as “Sono Shrine” and “Kara Shrine (two seats),” thus placed among the {{ill|Myōjin taisha|ja|名神大社|1=Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9610964}} and entitled to offerings at the Tsukinami and Niiname rites.{{Sfn|宮内省坐神三座（式内社）|1979年}} They are the only two shrines within the palace to be ranked as myōjin taisha. The ''Engishiki'' also stipulates that the shrines’ maintenance expenses come from the sealed households in Sanuki Province, and that one sealed servant (''fuchō'') be assigned to guard the shinden.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

The ''{{ill|Chōya Gunsai|ja|朝野群載|1=Chōya Gunsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517735}}'' records a petition for repairs in {{ill|Eihō|ja|永保|1=Eihō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193881}} 2 ({{ill|1082|ja|1082年|1=1082|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19452}}), from which the dilapidated state of the buildings can be inferred.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} According to the ''{{ill|Chōshūki|ja|長秋記|1=Minamoto no Morotoki's Diary|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653546}}''<ref group="原">''Chōshūki'', Daiji 5 (1130) 11/14 entry.</ref>, by around {{ill|Daiji (Japan)|ja|大治 (日本)|1=Daiji (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1037046}} 5 ({{ill|1130|ja|1130年|1=1130|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19562}}) the ancient form of the rites had been lost.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} The ''{{ill|Yasutomi-ki|ja|康富記|1=Yasutomi-ki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486253}}''<ref group="原">''Yasutomi-ki'', Ōei 26 (1419) 2/14 entry.</ref> notes that on the 5th day of the 2nd month of {{ill|Ōei|ja|応永|1=Ōei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193078}} 26 ({{ill|1419|ja|1419年|1=1419|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6205}}) a great wind toppled the shrine buildings.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} Thereafter, since the inner palace was destroyed in the Ōnin War, the Sonokarakami Shrine is likewise thought to have been abolished.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

It is unclear how the rites were continued after abolition, but it is thought that, together with the other palace deities, Sonokarakami is now venerated among the “Tenjin Chigi” in the ''Shinden'' of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿}} at the Imperial Palace. <ref>''Shikinai-sha Chōsa Hōkoku'' vol. 1, Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai ed., Kogakkan University Press, 1979, pp. 28–29.</ref>

=== Ranks ===
* {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥|1=Kashō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373157}} 3 ({{ill|850|ja|850年|1=850|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30364}}) 10/20: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower (''{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}'').<ref group="原" name="嘉祥3年">''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', Kashō 3 (850) 10th month, ''kinoe-ne'' (20th) entry (see {{Harvnb|薗神社（集成）}}; {{Harvnb|韓神社二座（集成）}}).</ref> — written as “Shrines of Sono-kami, Kara-kami, etc.”
* {{ill|Saikō|ja|斉衡|1=Saikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1324664}} 1 ({{ill|854|ja|854年|1=854|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24981}}) 4/9: Junior Third Rank (''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'').<ref group="原" name="斉衡元年">''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', Saikō 1 (854) 4th month, ''mizunoto-i'' (9th) entry (see {{Harvnb|薗神社（集成）}}; {{Harvnb|韓神社二座（集成）}}).</ref> — written as “Sono-kami” and “Kara-kami.”
* Saikō 2 ({{ill|855|ja|855年|1=855|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25073}}) 9/9: Listed as myōjin (''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'').<ref group="原" name="斉衡2年">''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', Saikō 2 (855) 9th month, ''mizunoto-chū'' (9th) entry (see {{Harvnb|薗神社（集成）}}; {{Harvnb|韓神社二座（集成）}}).</ref> — written as “Sono-kami” and “Kara-kami.”
* {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|1=Jōgan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1075635}} 1 ({{ill|859|ja|859年|1=859|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23897}}) 1/27: From Junior Third Rank to Senior Third Rank (''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'').<ref group="原" name="貞観元年">''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', Jōgan 1 (859) 1/27 entry (see {{Harvnb|薗神社（集成）}}; {{Harvnb|韓神社二座（集成）}}).</ref> — written as “Sono-kami” and “Kara-kami.”

== Rites ==
=== Sonokarakami Festival ===
The {{ill|annual festival|ja|例祭|1=Reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} of the Sonokarakami Shrine was called the “'''Sonokarakami no Matsuri'''.”{{Sfn|園韓神祭（国史）}} It was prescribed to be held twice a year: on the {{ill|Ox (zodiac)|ja|丑|1=chǒu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16539206}} day after the {{ill|Kasuga Festival|ja|春日祭|1=Kasuga-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513681}} in the 2nd month, and on the Ox day before the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501518}} in the 11th month.{{Sfn|園韓神祭（国史）}} The first record is from {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|1=Enryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373176}} 20 ({{ill|801|ja|801年|1=801|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30008}}),<ref group="原" name="延暦20年">''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku'' 1 “Keharaigoto,” Enryaku 20 (801) 5/14 order (see {{Harvnb|薗神社（集成）}}; {{Harvnb|韓神社二座（集成）}}).</ref><ref name="秦氏"/> and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that it was performed regularly.

The content of the rite is described in detail in the ''{{ill|Jōgan Gishiki|ja|貞観儀式|1=Jōgan Gishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634544}}'', etc.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} On the day, two ''kambe'' (神部, shrine officials) erected a sacred tree (''sakaki'') in the courtyard, lit a ritual fire, and the ''miko'' (御巫) recited norito. After the recitation, flute and ''koto'' were played, the ''mikoko'' (御神子) danced a ''yudate'' dance circling the fire, and then eight ''kambe'' danced together.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} The same sequence was then performed first at Sono Shrine, then at Kara Shrine, and afterwards a ''wa-mai'' dance was performed again at Sono Shrine.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} After the ministers and above had withdrawn, the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2264720}} performed singing and dancing (''kagura'') together with the ''miko'', ''monoimi'', and ''kambe'' in front of both sanctuaries.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} This ''kagura'' by the Jingikan, predating the ''Naishidokoro'' Kagura established in the time of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇|1=Emperor Ichijō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q449540}}, is considered a type of court ''kagura''.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

The ''Goke Shidai'' says four ''kambe'' danced holding ''sakaki'', halberds, bows, and swords, while the ''{{ill|Hyakurenshō|ja|百錬抄|1=Hyakurenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580870}}'' records that when they tried to retrieve the true images (''go-shōtai'') of the Sonokarakami during the Daidairi fire of {{ill|Daiji (Japan)|ja|大治 (日本)|1=Daiji (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1037046}} 2 ({{ill|1127|ja|1127年|1=1127|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23827}}), there were sacred treasures consisting of swords and halberds.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} Thereafter, the festival is said to have gradually declined after the late Heian period.{{Sfn|園韓神祭（国史）}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園韓神社]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園韓神社]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:園韓神社]]
[[File:Imperial palace.svg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Location of {{ill|Sonokarakami Shrine|ja|園韓神社|1=Sono & Kara Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422896}} in the {{ill|Daidairi|ja|大内裏|1=Heian Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304501}} of {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|1=Heian-kyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q751907}}}}]]
'''Sonokarakami Shrine''' (そのからかみのやしろ / Sonokarakami no yashiro, also Sonokarakami-sha) is a collective name for '''Sono Shrine''' (そのじんじゃ / Sono jinja, also 薗神社 ''Sono Jinja'') and '''Kara Shrine''' (からじんじゃ / Kara jinja, ''Karakami no yashiro''). It was a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}} ({{ill|Myōjin taisha|ja|名神大社|1=Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9610964}}) that is now defunct. It may have been succeeded by enshrinement in the ''Shinden'' of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿}}.

== Overview ==
Both Sono Shrine and Kara Shrine were {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrines|1=Shinto shrine}} that stood within the {{ill|宮内省|ja|宮内省|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193428}} of the imperial court ({{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|1=Heian-kyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q751907}}’s {{ill|Daidairi|ja|大内裏|1=Heian Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304501}}) until around the time of the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|1=Ōnin War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q385676}}.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} In the ''{{ill|Shūgaishō|ja|拾芥抄|1=Shūgaishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11497878}}'' palace plan they appear west of the Ministry of the Imperial Household, while in the ''{{ill|Nenjū Gyōji Emaki|ja|年中行事絵巻|1=Nenjū Gyōji Emaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17635666}}'' they appear at the ministry’s north-west corner; this spot corresponds to the north-east of Chikara-chō (主税町) in today’s {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|1=Kyoto Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q120730}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|1=Kyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q34600}}, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|上京区|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1321824}}.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} The shrine buildings were two halls aligned north–south (Sono Shrine to the south, Kara Shrine to the north), both facing east, each in a {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}-like style with one bay across the front and two bays on the sides.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

They were the only {{ill|Myōjin taisha|ja|名神大社|1=Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9610964}} within the palace precincts, and in the Heian period their {{ill|annual festival|ja|例祭|1=Reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}}—the “Sonokarakami no Matsuri”—was prescribed to be held twice a year, showing that the court regarded them as important. However, after the Ōnin War they were abolished, and to this day there are various theories regarding their divine identities and origins.

== Enshrined deities ==
As for the deity Sono-no-kami, there is no mention in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', etc., and the identity is unknown.{{Sfn|宮内省坐神三座（式内社）|1979年}} One theory says it was the deity guarding the garden pond (''sonochi'') of the Ministry of the Imperial Household.{{Sfn|宮内省坐神三座（式内社）|1979年}} Regarding Kara-no-kami, the ''Kojiki'' lists five divine offspring ({{ill|Mikogami|ja|御子神}}) of {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3028663}} and Inohime (daughter of {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神活須毘神}}): {{ill|Ōkuni Mitama no Kami|ja|大国御魂神}}, {{ill|Kara no Kami|ja|韓神}}, Sofuri no Kami ({{ill|曾富理神|ja|曾富理神}}), Shirahi no Kami ({{ill|白日神|ja|白日神}}), and {{ill|Hijiri no Kami|ja|聖神（日本神話）}}.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} (There is also a theory that this Kara no Kami is a different deity.{{Sfn|宮内省坐神三座（式内社）|1979年}}) {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|1=Motoori Norinaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q384647}}, in his ''{{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝|1=Kojiki-den|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410835}}'', proposed that Sofuri no Kami is identical with one of the two deities Sono-no-kami or Kara-no-kami.{{Sfn|園韓神祭（国史）}}

According to the ''Daiwa Jinja Chūshinjō'' (大倭神社註進状), Sono Shrine enshrined {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, while Kara Shrine enshrined {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}}; it further states that these deities were descendants of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q272993}} and protected against epidemics.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

== History ==
=== Foundation ===
The ''Daiwa Jinja Chūshinjō'', quoting the ''Ōgami Ujikechō'' (大神氏家牒), states that the shrines were founded by the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} in the {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老|1=Yōrō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373061}} ({{ill|717|ja|717年|1=717|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23532}}–{{ill|724|ja|724年|1=724|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23549}}).{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

On the other hand, according to the ''{{ill|Goke Shidai|ja|江家次第|1=Q11550820|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550820}}'' and ''{{ill|Kojidan|ja|古事談|1=Kojidan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3013995}}'', when it was planned to relocate the Sonokarakami Shrine at the time of the move to {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|1=Heian-kyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q751907}}, a divine oracle declared: “We will remain here to protect the emperor,” and so the relocation was cancelled.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} From this account it is thought that from its foundation the shrine stood near the Ministry of the Imperial Household, and it is said to be the oldest among the 36 deities of the palace.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} Because the area before the relocation to Heian-kyō had been governed by the immigrant {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|1=Hata clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q987290}}, there is also a theory that Sono-no-kami and Kara-no-kami were originally deities venerated by the Hata. <ref name="秦氏">{{ill|Chiaki Mizutani|ja|水谷千秋|1=Chiaki Mizutani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549157}}『謎の渡来人秦氏（文春新書）』{{ill|Bungeishunjū|ja|文藝春秋|1=Bungeishunjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q906927}}, 2009, pp. 206–212.</ref>

[[File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kankoku Shrine|ja|漢國神社|1=Kangō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11565493}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|1=Nara Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131287}}, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}})}}{{Smaller|According to its shrine legend, it is the source of the Sonokarakami Shrine’s enshrinement.}}]]
In another account, the shrine legend of {{ill|Kankoku Shrine|ja|漢國神社|1=Kangō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11565493}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|1=Nara Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131287}} says that during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko|2=ja|3=推古天皇|ja|推古天皇|1=Suiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q298057}} Ōgami no Kimi Shirotsutsumi (大神君白堤) enshrined Ōmononushi as Sono-no-kami, and later, in the Yōrō era, {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|ja|藤原不比等|1=Fujiwara no Fuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q177517}} enshrined Ōkuninushi and Sukunabikona as Kara-no-kami.{{Sfn|漢国神社（国史）}} It further states that the imperial court’s enshrinement of these deities became the foundation of the Sonokarakami Shrine.{{Sfn|漢国神社（国史）}}

=== Outline history ===
In documents, the earliest mention is in the ''{{ill|Shinsō kyakuchiku shō|ja|新抄格勅符抄|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502312}}''<ref group="原">''Shinsō kyakuchiku shō'' 10, “Shinji Shoke Fuko,” Daidō 1 (806) document.</ref>, which records that in {{ill|Tenpyō-jingo|ja|天平神護|1=Tenpyō-jingo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373181}} 1 ({{ill|765|ja|765年|1=765|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q31216}}) 20 households of {{ill|shinpu|ja|神封|1=Shinpo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1689534}} (fief households) were allotted from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|1=Sanuki Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q929682}} to Sono-no-kami and 10 to Kara-no-kami. The ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格}}''<ref group="原" name="延暦20年"/> notes the Sonokarakami festival as an {{ill|annual festival|ja|例祭|1=Reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} in {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|1=Enryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373176}} 20 ({{ill|801|ja|801年|1=801|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30008}}).

In the official histories, in {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥|1=Kashō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373157}} 3 ({{ill|850|ja|850年|1=850|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30364}}){{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}<ref group="原" name="嘉祥3年"/> the shrines were granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower; in {{ill|Saikō|ja|斉衡|1=Saikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1324664}} 1 ({{ill|854|ja|854年|1=854|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24981}}){{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}<ref group="原" name="斉衡元年"/> they were raised to Junior Third Rank; in Saikō 2 ({{ill|855|ja|855年|1=855|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25073}}){{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}<ref group="原" name="斉衡2年"/> they were listed as {{ill|myōjin|ja|名神|1=myoujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415523}}; and in {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|1=Jōgan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1075635}} 1 ({{ill|859|ja|859年|1=859|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23897}}){{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}<ref group="原" name="貞観元年"/> they were promoted from Junior Third Rank to Senior Third Rank.

In the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'' ''{{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11064932}}'' compiled in {{ill|927|ja|927年|1=927|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30470}}, under “Three Deities Enshrined in the Ministry of the Imperial Household; all myōjin; for the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516161}} and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501518}},” they are listed as “Sono Shrine” and “Kara Shrine (two seats),” thus placed among the {{ill|Myōjin taisha|ja|名神大社|1=Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9610964}} and entitled to offerings at the Tsukinami and Niiname rites.{{Sfn|宮内省坐神三座（式内社）|1979年}} They are the only two shrines within the palace to be ranked as myōjin taisha. The ''Engishiki'' also stipulates that the shrines’ maintenance expenses come from the sealed households in Sanuki Province, and that one sealed servant (''fuchō'') be assigned to guard the shinden.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

The ''{{ill|Chōya Gunsai|ja|朝野群載|1=Chōya Gunsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517735}}'' records a petition for repairs in {{ill|Eihō|ja|永保|1=Eihō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193881}} 2 ({{ill|1082|ja|1082年|1=1082|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19452}}), from which the dilapidated state of the buildings can be inferred.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} According to the ''{{ill|Chōshūki|ja|長秋記|1=Minamoto no Morotoki's Diary|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653546}}''<ref group="原">''Chōshūki'', Daiji 5 (1130) 11/14 entry.</ref>, by around {{ill|Daiji (Japan)|ja|大治 (日本)|1=Daiji (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1037046}} 5 ({{ill|1130|ja|1130年|1=1130|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19562}}) the ancient form of the rites had been lost.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} The ''{{ill|Yasutomi-ki|ja|康富記|1=Yasutomi-ki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486253}}''<ref group="原">''Yasutomi-ki'', Ōei 26 (1419) 2/14 entry.</ref> notes that on the 5th day of the 2nd month of {{ill|Ōei|ja|応永|1=Ōei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193078}} 26 ({{ill|1419|ja|1419年|1=1419|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6205}}) a great wind toppled the shrine buildings.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} Thereafter, since the inner palace was destroyed in the Ōnin War, the Sonokarakami Shrine is likewise thought to have been abolished.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

It is unclear how the rites were continued after abolition, but it is thought that, together with the other palace deities, Sonokarakami is now venerated among the “Tenjin Chigi” in the ''Shinden'' of the {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja|宮中三殿}} at the Imperial Palace. <ref>''Shikinai-sha Chōsa Hōkoku'' vol. 1, Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai ed., Kogakkan University Press, 1979, pp. 28–29.</ref>

=== Ranks ===
* {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥|1=Kashō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373157}} 3 ({{ill|850|ja|850年|1=850|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30364}}) 10/20: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower (''{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}'').<ref group="原" name="嘉祥3年">''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', Kashō 3 (850) 10th month, ''kinoe-ne'' (20th) entry (see {{Harvnb|薗神社（集成）}}; {{Harvnb|韓神社二座（集成）}}).</ref> — written as “Shrines of Sono-kami, Kara-kami, etc.”
* {{ill|Saikō|ja|斉衡|1=Saikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1324664}} 1 ({{ill|854|ja|854年|1=854|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24981}}) 4/9: Junior Third Rank (''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'').<ref group="原" name="斉衡元年">''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', Saikō 1 (854) 4th month, ''mizunoto-i'' (9th) entry (see {{Harvnb|薗神社（集成）}}; {{Harvnb|韓神社二座（集成）}}).</ref> — written as “Sono-kami” and “Kara-kami.”
* Saikō 2 ({{ill|855|ja|855年|1=855|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25073}}) 9/9: Listed as myōjin (''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'').<ref group="原" name="斉衡2年">''Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', Saikō 2 (855) 9th month, ''mizunoto-chū'' (9th) entry (see {{Harvnb|薗神社（集成）}}; {{Harvnb|韓神社二座（集成）}}).</ref> — written as “Sono-kami” and “Kara-kami.”
* {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観 (日本)|1=Jōgan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1075635}} 1 ({{ill|859|ja|859年|1=859|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23897}}) 1/27: From Junior Third Rank to Senior Third Rank (''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'').<ref group="原" name="貞観元年">''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', Jōgan 1 (859) 1/27 entry (see {{Harvnb|薗神社（集成）}}; {{Harvnb|韓神社二座（集成）}}).</ref> — written as “Sono-kami” and “Kara-kami.”

== Rites ==
=== Sonokarakami Festival ===
The {{ill|annual festival|ja|例祭|1=Reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} of the Sonokarakami Shrine was called the “'''Sonokarakami no Matsuri'''.”{{Sfn|園韓神祭（国史）}} It was prescribed to be held twice a year: on the {{ill|Ox (zodiac)|ja|丑|1=chǒu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16539206}} day after the {{ill|Kasuga Festival|ja|春日祭|1=Kasuga-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513681}} in the 2nd month, and on the Ox day before the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501518}} in the 11th month.{{Sfn|園韓神祭（国史）}} The first record is from {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|1=Enryaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373176}} 20 ({{ill|801|ja|801年|1=801|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30008}}),<ref group="原" name="延暦20年">''Ruijū Sandai Kyaku'' 1 “Keharaigoto,” Enryaku 20 (801) 5/14 order (see {{Harvnb|薗神社（集成）}}; {{Harvnb|韓神社二座（集成）}}).</ref><ref name="秦氏"/> and ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that it was performed regularly.

The content of the rite is described in detail in the ''{{ill|Jōgan Gishiki|ja|貞観儀式|1=Jōgan Gishiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634544}}'', etc.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} On the day, two ''kambe'' (神部, shrine officials) erected a sacred tree (''sakaki'') in the courtyard, lit a ritual fire, and the ''miko'' (御巫) recited norito. After the recitation, flute and ''koto'' were played, the ''mikoko'' (御神子) danced a ''yudate'' dance circling the fire, and then eight ''kambe'' danced together.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} The same sequence was then performed first at Sono Shrine, then at Kara Shrine, and afterwards a ''wa-mai'' dance was performed again at Sono Shrine.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} After the ministers and above had withdrawn, the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2264720}} performed singing and dancing (''kagura'') together with the ''miko'', ''monoimi'', and ''kambe'' in front of both sanctuaries.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} This ''kagura'' by the Jingikan, predating the ''Naishidokoro'' Kagura established in the time of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇|1=Emperor Ichijō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q449540}}, is considered a type of court ''kagura''.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}}

The ''Goke Shidai'' says four ''kambe'' danced holding ''sakaki'', halberds, bows, and swords, while the ''{{ill|Hyakurenshō|ja|百錬抄|1=Hyakurenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580870}}'' records that when they tried to retrieve the true images (''go-shōtai'') of the Sonokarakami during the Daidairi fire of {{ill|Daiji (Japan)|ja|大治 (日本)|1=Daiji (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1037046}} 2 ({{ill|1127|ja|1127年|1=1127|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23827}}), there were sacred treasures consisting of swords and halberds.{{Sfn|園韓神社跡（平凡社）|1979年}} Thereafter, the festival is said to have gradually declined after the late Heian period.{{Sfn|園韓神祭（国史）}}
